title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. thuretii
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. thuretii, also known as the Scotch rose or alpine rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subsp. thuretii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections.
